sanskrit:
एवं बहुविधा यज्ञा वितता ब्रह्मणो मुखे।
कर्मजान्विद्धि तान्सर्वानेवं ज्ञात्वा विमोक्ष्यसे॥
transliteration:
evaṃ bahu-vidhā yajñā vitatā brahmaṇo mukhe |
karma-jān viddhi tān sarvān evaṃ jñātvā vimokṣyase ||
literal_meaning: Thus manifold sacrifices are spread forth from the mouth of the Veda; know them all as born of action; thus knowing, you shall be freed.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reassures Arjuna that every legitimate life-path—whether physical, mental, or intellectual—is a valid ‘sacrifice’ that can culminate in liberation if performed with the right understanding.
psychological_state: Cognitive relief from existential perfectionism; permission to use one’s natural temperament as a vehicle for growth rather than renouncing it.
trigger_scenario: A young professional torn between ‘spiritual’ Instagram ideals and their demanding career realizes their daily work can itself become mindful service.
anchor_text: Every honest effort becomes a path to freedom when done as selfless service.
